DP.371.0000:
Oh, hello!
Right this way, please!\f
DP.371.0001:
Hello.
I’m glad to meet you.\f
My name is Teala, and I’m your guide
to the Pokémon Wi-Fi Club.\f
First, I need to give you this.\f
DP.371.0002:
That’s your Pal Pad. You may register
your friends in it.\f
Once registered, you may link with
those friends over Nintendo WFC\f
to trade Pokémon, battle,
and so on.\f
Would you like to know more?
DP.371.0003:
You may meet people on the second
floor of a Pokémon Center for trades\r
or Colosseum battles.\f
You may register such people as
friends in your Pal Pad.\f
You may also manually register friends
by entering their Friend Codes into\r
your Pal Pad.\f
Would you like me to repeat that?
DP.371.0004:
I hope you enjoy using Nintendo
WFC!
DP.371.0005:
Welcome to the Pokémon Center Wi-Fi
Club!\f
May I help you with anything?
DP.371.0006:
Let me explain how the Pokémon Wi-Fi
Club is set up.\f
From the center counter, you may
use Nintendo WFC.\f
Over Nintendo WFC, you may
conduct trades and battles with\f
friends that you have registered
in your Pal Pad.
DP.371.0007:
Welcome to the Pokémon Wi-Fi Club!\f
Would you like to use Nintendo WFC?
DP.371.0009:
Would you like to launch
Nintendo WFC?
DP.371.0011:
...Oh?\f
I’m sorry, but you don’t have any
friends registered in your Pal Pad.\f
The Wi-Fi Club cannot be used if
no friends are in your Pal Pad.\f
Would you like to get your own
Friend Code at least?
DP.371.0012:
...Oh?\f
I’m sorry, but you don’t have any
friends registered in your Pal Pad.\f
The Wi-Fi Club cannot be used if
no friends are in your Pal Pad.\f
Please register friends in your Pal
Pad, and then come back.
